devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एवं ते भगवद्दूता यमदूताभिभाषितम् । उपधार्याथ तान् राजन् प्रत्याहुर्नयकोविदाः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaevaṃ te bhagavaddūtā yamadūtābhibhāṣitamupadhāryātha tān rājan pratyāhurnayakovidāḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having thus carefully heard the exposition addressed (to them) by the messengers of Yama, the angels of Lord Viṣṇu who were pastmasters in philosophy and ethics, replied to them, Oh King.

devanagari: श्रीविष्णुदूता ऊचुः । अहो कष्टं धर्मदृशां अधर्मः स्पृशते सभाम् । यत्रादण्ड्येष्वपापेषु दण्डो यैर्ध्रियते वृथा ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīviṣṇudūtā ūcuḥaho kaṣṭaṃ dharmadṛśāṃ adharmaḥ spṛśate sabhāmyatrādaṇḍyeṣvapāpeṣu daṇḍo yairdhriyate vṛthā
translation:
The messengers of Viṣṇu said:
Oh What a pity! Injustice (or unrighteousness) influences the assembly of those who should see through (the real nature of (righteousness. There, punishment is wrongly inflicted by these (custodians of righteousness) on persons who are not sinners and do not (hence) deserve it.

devanagari: प्रजानां पितरो ये च शास्तारः साधवः समाः । यदि स्यात्तेषु वैषम्यं कं यान्ति शरणं प्रजाः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: prajānāṃ pitaro ye ca śāstāraḥ sādhavaḥ samāḥyadi syātteṣu vaiṣamyaṃ kaṃ yānti śaraṇaṃ prajāḥ
translation: To whom can created beings go for a shelter, if injustice particularly enters into their fatherly protectors and teachers like rulers who are expected to be well-disposed, righteous and impartial?

devanagari: यद् यद् आचरति श्रेयान् इतरः तत् तदीहते । स यत्प्रमाणं कुरुते लोकस्तदनुवर्तते ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: yad yad ācarati śreyān itaraḥ tat tadīhatesa yatpramāṇaṃ kurute lokastadanuvartate
translation: [1] Whatever a superior person does is emulated by other common people. Whatever he sets up as a standard or regards as an authority the masses (ordinary people) follow it.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
A verbatim quotation from Bhagavad Gītā 3.21.

devanagari: यस्याङ्के शिर आधाय लोकः स्वपिति निर्वृतः । स्वयं धर्ममधर्मं वा न हि वेद यथा पशुः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: yasyāṅke śira ādhāya lokaḥ svapiti nirvṛtaḥsvayaṃ dharmamadharmaṃ vā na hi veda yathā paśuḥ
translation: A common man, like unto a brute, does not understand, of his own accord, what is righteousness and unrighteousness. Placing his head confidently on the lap (of a fatherly- protector like Yama-dharma), he sleeps resting without any anxiety.

devanagari: स कथं न्यर्पितात्मानं कृतमैत्रमचेतनम् । विस्रम्भणीयो भूतानां सघृणो द्रोग्धुमर्हति ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: sa kathaṃ nyarpitātmānaṃ kṛtamaitramacetanamvisrambhaṇīyo bhūtānāṃ saghṛṇo drogdhumarhati
translation: How is it proper that if the latter is really deserving of confidence of all beings and has some compassion (and scruples), he can be so treacherous to one who has completely surrendered himself in faith, and cherishes friendship, and is unaware (verging on unconsciousness).

devanagari: अयं हि कृतनिर्वेशो जन्मकोट्यंहसामपि । यद् व्याजहार विवशो नाम स्वस्त्ययनं हरेः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: ayaṃ hi kṛtanirveśo janmakoṭyaṃhasāmapiyad vyājahāra vivaśo nāma svastyayanaṃ hareḥ
translation: That this man (Ajāmila), even in a helpless stage uttered the name of Hari which (not only expiates sins but) leads to the Final Beatitude, has atoned for all the sins committed by him even in crores of his past incarnations.

devanagari: एतेनैव ह्यघोनोऽस्य कृतं स्यादघनिष्कृतम् । यदा नारायणायेति जगाद चतुरक्षरम् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: etenaiva hyaghono'sya kṛtaṃ syādaghaniṣkṛtamyadā nārāyaṇāyeti jagāda caturakṣaram
translation: For, by the very act that he uttered the four syllables NĀ—RĀ—YA—ṆA (while calling his son ‘Oh Nārāyaṇa come’), this sinner has already completely atoned for all his sins.

devanagari: स्तेनः सुरापो मित्रध्रुग् ब्रह्महा गुरुतल्पगः । स्त्रीराजपितृगोहन्ता ये च पातकिनोऽपरे ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: stenaḥ surāpo mitradhrug brahmahā gurutalpagaḥstrīrājapitṛgohantā ye ca pātakino'pare
translation: [2] A thief, a wine-drinker, perpetrator of treachery to one’s friend, a murderer of a Brāhmaṇa, one who violates his preceptor’s bed (i.e. has illicit relation with one’s preceptor’s wife), the slayer of a woman, a king, a parent or a cow and other types of sinners—to all these sinners, the utterance of the name of Viṣṇu is the most effective expiation, for Lord’s mind gets attracted to the sinner uttering his name (or the sinner’s mind is attracted to the Lord).
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā and VC. write long discourses on the importance of God’s name which may be read in the original. V.J. points out that the condition precedent is that the name of the Lord is to be uttered with ardent devotion.

devanagari: सर्वेषां अप्यघवतां इदमेव सुनिष्कृतम् । नामव्याहरणं विष्णोः यतस्तद् विषया मतिः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: sarveṣāṃ apyaghavatāṃ idameva suniṣkṛtamnāmavyāharaṇaṃ viṣṇoḥ yatastad viṣayā matiḥ
translation: [2] A thief, a wine-drinker, perpetrator of treachery to one’s friend, a murderer of a Brāhmaṇa, one who violates his preceptor’s bed (i.e. has illicit relation with one’s preceptor’s wife), the slayer of a woman, a king, a parent or a cow and other types of sinners—to all these sinners, the utterance of the name of Viṣṇu is the most effective expiation, for Lord’s mind gets attracted to the sinner uttering his name (or the sinner’s mind is attracted to the Lord).
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā and VC. write long discourses on the importance of God’s name which may be read in the original. V.J. points out that the condition precedent is that the name of the Lord is to be uttered with ardent devotion.

devanagari: न निष्कृतैरुदितैर्ब्रह्मवादिभिः । तथा विशुद्ध्यत्यघवान् व्रतादिभिः । यथा हरेर्नामपदैरुदाहृतैः । तदुत्तमश्लोक गुणोपलम्भकम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: na niṣkṛtairuditairbrahmavādibhiḥtathā viśuddhyatyaghavān vratādibhiḥyathā harernāmapadairudāhṛtaiḥtaduttamaśloka guṇopalambhakam
translation: A sinner is not so thoroughly purified to that extent, by performing the expiatory acts and observances of vows prescribed by the masters of the Vedic lore (like Manu and others), as by the utterance of the mere syllables of the names of Hari (without adding ‘I bow to’), as his name is suggestive of the excellences of the glorious Lord. (The effect of Lord’s name does not exhaust and disappear immediately after dispelling the sin as is the case with the penances like Kṛcchra and Cāndrāyaṇa.[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
VC. adds—This is the omnipotence of the Almighty Lord.

devanagari: नैकान्तिकं तद्धि कृतेऽपि निष्कृते । मनः पुनर्धावति चेदसत्पथे । तत्कर्मनिर्हारमभीप्सतां हरेः । गुणानुवादः खलु सत्त्वभावनः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: naikāntikaṃ taddhi kṛte'pi niṣkṛtemanaḥ punardhāvati cedasatpathetatkarmanirhāramabhīpsatāṃ hareḥguṇānuvādaḥ khalu sattvabhāvanaḥ
translation: For if the mind again runs (is attracted) to the evil path even after going through the process of expiation, that act of atonement is not thorough (in destroying sin). For those who desire to annihilate (the effects of) their (sinful) acts, repeatedly extolling the merits (by uttering the names) of Hari is the sure remedy, as it (actually) purifies the mind.

devanagari: अथैनं मापनयत कृताशेषाघनिष्कृतम् । यदसौ भगवन्नाम म्रियमाणः समग्रहीत् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: athainaṃ māpanayata kṛtāśeṣāghaniṣkṛtamyadasau bhagavannāma mriyamāṇaḥ samagrahīt
translation: Hence, do not carry him (the jīva of Ajāmila) by the wrong way (the sinner’s path leading to Yama’s abode). He has completely expiated all his sins. (When even a syllable of the Lord’s name is sufficient to annihilate all sins), he has uttered the complete name of the Lord when he was about to die (and there is no time and hence possibility of committing a sin after articulating the Lord’s name).

devanagari: साङ्केत्यं पारिहास्यं वा स्तोभं हेलनमेव वा । वैकुण्ठनामग्रहणं अशेषाघहरं विदुः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: sāṅketyaṃ pārihāsyaṃ vā stobhaṃ helanameva vāvaikuṇṭhanāmagrahaṇaṃ aśeṣāghaharaṃ viduḥ
translation: (As to the objection that Ajāmila called his son and and not Viṣṇu, Viṣṇu’s messengers explain). The experts in Śāstras say that utterance of the Lord’s name even if taken to denote another person, or in a friendly joke or as an insertion during the recitation of a song, or in (an outward, apparent) disrespect[4], completely annihilates all sins.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
But real disrespect for God’s name is a great sin.

devanagari: पतितः स्खलितो भग्नः सन्दष्टस्तप्त आहतः । हरिरित्यवशेनाह पुमान्नार्हति यातनाम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: patitaḥ skhalito bhagnaḥ sandaṣṭastapta āhataḥharirityavaśenāha pumānnārhati yātanām
translation: If a person unconsciously (and involuntarily) utters the Lord’s name (Hari or any other) while he falls, tumbles down, gets maimed, is bitten (by a serpent), heated (by fever etc.) and struck (by a stick), he does not deserve to be tormented in hell.

devanagari: गुरूणां च लघूनां च गुरूणि च लघूनि च । प्रायश्चित्तानि पापानां ज्ञात्वोक्तानि महर्षिभिः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: gurūṇāṃ ca laghūnāṃ ca gurūṇi ca laghūni caprāyaścittāni pāpānāṃ jñātvoktāni maharṣibhiḥ
translation: (Proportionately) heavy (difficult) and easy processes of atonement for sins of grievous and light natures have been thought over and laid down by the great sages (like Manu and other writers on the Dharma-Śāstra). (But there is no such differentiation in case of Lord’s name, as by merely remembering Lord Viṣṇu, one is absolved of all sins).

devanagari: तैस्तान्यघानि पूयन्ते तपोदानजपादिभिः । नाधर्मजं तद् हृदयं तदपीशाङ्घ्रिसेवया ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: taistānyaghāni pūyante tapodānajapādibhiḥnādharmajaṃ tad hṛdayaṃ tadapīśāṅghrisevayā
translation:
Those (different kinds of) sins are washed away by the austerities, charitable acts, mutterings (of prayers or mantras) and such other (expiatory) processes. But the heart of the sinner (or the subtle residual forms of the sins called impressions or saṃskāras) are not thereby purified. Even that is achieved by the service of the feet of the Lord. (All the sins are annihilated by the very name of Hari, but vāsanā or the impressions unconsciously left on the mind by past actions are wiped out by the vision of Lord Viṣṇu—the Great Man etc.).
17a[5]. This Dharma, characterised by the repetition of the name of the Lord, is the highest and the ancientmost, and has been thus laid down. If practised, it purifies the mind day by day, like clothes washed on every day.
17b[5]. As the individual soul (jīva) has no independence in relation to the acts he performs, the expiatory acts cannot completely purify the jīvas mind (due to his dependence on God’s will). Desisting from forbidden acts, he should therefore worship Hari by continuous singing and repetition of his name.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
17a & 17b are additional verses in Padaratnāvalī’s text.

Footnote [5]:
17a & 17b are additional verses in Padaratnāvalī’s text.

devanagari: अज्ञानादथवा ज्ञानात् उत्तमश्लोकनाम यत् । सङ्कीर्तितमघं पुंसो दहेदेधो यथानलः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: ajñānādathavā jñānāt uttamaślokanāma yatsaṅkīrtitamaghaṃ puṃso dahededho yathānalaḥ
translation: Irrespective of a man’s knowledge or ignorance (about the efficacy and potentiality of the Lord’s name for purifying thoroughly all the sins), if Lord Viṣṇu’s name is sung or muttered repeatedly, it burns down all the sins of man, like fire (even though ignorantly thrown by a child on a heap of fuel) burns the fuel completely.

devanagari: यथागदं वीर्यतमं उपयुक्तं यदृच्छया । अजानतोऽप्यात्मगुणं कुर्यान् मंत्रोऽप्युदाहृतः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: yathāgadaṃ vīryatamaṃ upayuktaṃ yadṛcchayāajānato'pyātmaguṇaṃ kuryān maṃtro'pyudāhṛtaḥ
translation:
Just as a medicinal drug of the highest potency, even though taken by chance, without being aware of its efficacy, is bound to show its remedial effects, similarly the sacred formula (mantra) consisting of the Lord’s name, even when muttered by chance, without knowing its highest purificatory power) shows its efficacy (in annihilating all sins). (Inherent power or potency does not require faith etc. in showing its efficacy).
19a.[6] Oh messengers (of Yama)! If you still entertain any doubt about the righteousness (of this), you consult (enquire) with your Lord, for the glorious god Yama knows the most secrets of (what is the real) Dharma.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
An additional verse in the text of Bhāgavata Candrikā and Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । ते एवं सुविनिर्णीय धर्मं भागवतं नृप । तं याम्यपाशान्निर्मुच्य विप्रं मृत्योरमूमुचन् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācate evaṃ suvinirṇīya dharmaṃ bhāgavataṃ nṛpataṃ yāmyapāśānnirmucya vipraṃ mṛtyoramūmucan
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Oh King! Having thus logically and convincingly explained the path of the Lord (the Bhāgavata dharma) and after liberating him (Ajāmila’s jīva) from the noose of Yama, they (Viṣṇu’s messengers) got him released from (the clutches of) Death.

devanagari: इति प्रत्युदिता याम्या दूता यात्वा यमान्तिके । यमराज्ञे यथा सर्वं आचचक्षुररिन्दम ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: iti pratyuditā yāmyā dūtā yātvā yamāntikeyamarājñe yathā sarvaṃ ācacakṣurarindama
translation: The messengers of Yama who were thus (discomfited and) expelled, approached god Yama and faithfully reported the whole matter to King Yamadharma, Oh vanquisher of enemies!

devanagari: द्विजः पाशाद्विनिर्मुक्तो गतभीः प्रकृतिं गतः । ववन्दे शिरसा विष्णोः किङ्करान् दर्शनोत्सवः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: dvijaḥ pāśādvinirmukto gatabhīḥ prakṛtiṃ gataḥvavande śirasā viṣṇoḥ kiṅkarān darśanotsavaḥ
translation: Being completely extricated from the noose of Yama, the Brāhmaṇa overcame his fear, and became his own former normal self.[7] Feeling delighted at their sight, he bowed to the servants of Viṣṇu with his head.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Resumed his previous pious way of life.—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: तं विवक्षुमभिप्रेत्य महापुरुषकिङ्कराः । सहसा पश्यतस्तस्य तत्रान्तर्दधिरेऽनघ ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ vivakṣumabhipretya mahāpuruṣakiṅkarāḥsahasā paśyatastasya tatrāntardadhire'nagha
translation: Perceiving that he was desirous to speak to them, the servants of Lord Viṣṇu instantaneously disappeared on that very spot, while he(Ajāmila) was (simply) looking on, Oh sinless King.

devanagari: अजामिलोऽप्यथाकर्ण्य दूतानां यमकृष्णयोः । धर्मं भागवतं शुद्धं त्रैवेद्यं च गुणाश्रयम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: ajāmilo'pyathākarṇya dūtānāṃ yamakṛṣṇayoḥdharmaṃ bhāgavataṃ śuddhaṃ traivedyaṃ ca guṇāśrayam
translation: Ajāmila also heard separately from the conversation of the messengers of Yama and of Viṣṇu (Kṛṣṇa), the (ritualistic) Dharma as propounded in three Vedas and based on guṇas (as the fruit of this Dharma, viz. celestial pleasures are a product of guṇas), and also the Pure Dharma pertaining to (and consisting of absolute devotion to) the Lord, and transcending the three guṇas.

devanagari: भक्तिमान्भगवत्याशु माहात्म्यश्रवणाद्धरेः । अनुतापो महानासीत्स्मरतोऽशुभमात्मनः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: bhaktimānbhagavatyāśu māhātmyaśravaṇāddhareḥanutāpo mahānāsītsmarato'śubhamātmanaḥ
translation: Having heard the great glories of Lord Hari, he immediately conceived earnest devotion to the Lord. Remembering his evil past, he felt deeply remorsed.

devanagari: अहो मे परमं कष्टं अभूद् अविजितात्मनः । येन विप्लावितं ब्रह्म वृषल्यां जायतात्मना ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: aho me paramaṃ kaṣṭaṃ abhūd avijitātmanaḥyena viplāvitaṃ brahma vṛṣalyāṃ jāyatātmanā
translation: “Alas! How extremely deplorable it was of me who could not subdue my (lower) Self, and procreating myself in the form of sons from a Śūdra woman, I have desecreted my Brahmin-hood.

devanagari: धिङ्मां विगर्हितं सद्भिः दुष्कृतं कुलकज्जलम् । हित्वा बालां सतीं योऽहं सुरापीमसतीमगाम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: dhiṅmāṃ vigarhitaṃ sadbhiḥ duṣkṛtaṃ kulakajjalamhitvā bālāṃ satīṃ yo'haṃ surāpīmasatīmagām
translation: Fie upon me who am condemned by good people as a sinful wretch, a dark stigma on my family—I who abandoned my virtuous young wife, and went to an unchaste wench given to drinking.

devanagari: वृद्धावनाथौ पितरौ नान्यबन्धू तपस्विनौ । अहो मयाधुना त्यक्तौ अकृतज्ञेन नीचवत् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: vṛddhāvanāthau pitarau nānyabandhū tapasvinauaho mayādhunā tyaktau akṛtajñena nīcavat
translation: Alas! An ungrateful vile fellow that I am, I have forthwith deserted my supportless, poor, aged, afflicted parents who had no relative to protect them.

devanagari: सोऽहं व्यक्तं पतिष्यामि नरके भृशदारुणे । धर्मघ्नाः कामिनो यत्र विन्दन्ति यमयातनाः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: so'haṃ vyaktaṃ patiṣyāmi narake bhṛśadāruṇedharmaghnāḥ kāmino yatra vindanti yamayātanāḥ
translation: I, who am of that nature, shall obviously fall into that terrible-most hell wherein lustful violaters of the path of righteousness undergo tortures inflicted by Yama.

devanagari: किमिदं स्वप्न आहो स्वित् साक्षाद् दृष्टमिहाद्‍भुतम् । क्व याता अद्य ते ये मां व्यकर्षन् पाशपाणयः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: kimidaṃ svapna āho svit sākṣād dṛṣṭamihād‍bhutamkva yātā adya te ye māṃ vyakarṣan pāśapāṇayaḥ
translation: Was this a dream or was it a reality that I witnessed a miracle here? Where have the noose-bearers who dragged me (out of my body) today, gone now?

devanagari: अथ ते क्व गताः सिद्धाः चत्वारश्चारुदर्शनाः । व्यामोचयन् नीयमानं बद्ध्वा पाशैरधो भुवः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: atha te kva gatāḥ siddhāḥ catvāraścārudarśanāḥvyāmocayan nīyamānaṃ baddhvā pāśairadho bhuvaḥ
translation: Again, where have gone those four beautiful-looking Siddhas (demi-gods) who released me while I was being taken noose-bound, down to the subterranean (infernal) regions?

devanagari: अथापि मे दुर्भगस्य विबुधोत्तमदर्शने । भवितव्यं मङ्गलेन येनात्मा मे प्रसीदति ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: athāpi me durbhagasya vibudhottamadarśanebhavitavyaṃ maṅgalena yenātmā me prasīdati
translation: In spite of my being a wretched person (in this birth), there must have been some auspicious (deed done in previous birth) inasmuch as I had the vision of those foremost gods and due to which my mind feels serene and peaceful now.

devanagari: अन्यथा म्रियमाणस्य नाशुचेर्वृषलीपतेः । वैकुण्ठनामग्रहणं जिह्वा वक्तुमिहार्हति ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: anyathā mriyamāṇasya nāśucervṛṣalīpateḥvaikuṇṭhanāmagrahaṇaṃ jihvā vaktumihārhati
translation: Otherwise (but for such auspicious acts in my previous birth) the tongue of an impure, sinner like me who kept a Śūdra woman, could be capable of uttering, at the time of death, the name of Lord Viṣṇu.

devanagari: क्व चाहं कितवः पापो ब्रह्मघ्नो निरपत्रपः । क्व च नारायणेत्येतद् भगवन्नाम मङ्गलम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: kva cāhaṃ kitavaḥ pāpo brahmaghno nirapatrapaḥkva ca nārāyaṇetyetad bhagavannāma maṅgalam
translation: Oh! What a contrast between a shameless rogue and a sinner like me! I have violated (destroyed) my Brāhmaṇahood and the auspicious name of the Lord ‘Nārāyaṇa’.

devanagari: सोऽहं तथा यतिष्यामि यतचित्तेन्द्रियानिलः । यथा न भूय आत्मानं अन्धे तमसि मज्जये ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: so'haṃ tathā yatiṣyāmi yatacittendriyānilaḥyathā na bhūya ātmānaṃ andhe tamasi majjaye
translation: Though I be such, I who have controlled my mind, cognitive and conative organs and breath, shall endeavour that way whereby I shall not plunge myself in the blinding -darkness in the form of transmigration of the Soul.

devanagari: विमुच्य तमिमं बन्धं अविद्या कामकर्मजम् । सर्वभूतसुहृच्छान्तो मैत्रः करुण आत्मवान् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: vimucya tamimaṃ bandhaṃ avidyā kāmakarmajamsarvabhūtasuhṛcchānto maitraḥ karuṇa ātmavān
translation: Having freed myself from the bonds (for household and relatives) due to avidyā (wrong identification of the body with the Soul), lust (for enjoyment of pleasures) and actions (binding the doer to this world and the next), I shall be a friend to all beings, serene, friendly, compassionate and self-controlled.

devanagari: मोचये ग्रस्तमात्मानं योषिन्मय्याऽत्ममायया । विक्रीडितो ययैवाहं क्रीडामृग इवाधमः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: mocaye grastamātmānaṃ yoṣinmayyā'tmamāyayāvikrīḍito yayaivāhaṃ krīḍāmṛga ivādhamaḥ
translation: I shall liberate myself who am completely possessed by Lord’s Māyā in the form of a woman (my Śūdra keep) by whom, wretched as I was, I was made to dance and play like a deer kept for amusement.

devanagari: ममाहमिति देहादौ हित्वामिथ्यार्थधीर्मतिम् । धास्ये मनो भगवति शुद्धं तत्कीर्तनादिभिः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: mamāhamiti dehādau hitvāmithyārthadhīrmatimdhāsye mano bhagavati śuddhaṃ tatkīrtanādibhiḥ
translation: Having given up the notion of ‘I’ and‘mine’ with reference to my body, (belongings, property etc.) and with my thought concentrated on the real substance, I shall fix my mind on the Lord. My mind has been purified by singing of the name of the Lord (and other modes of bhakti).”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इति जातसुनिर्वेदः क्षणसङ्गेन साधुषु । गङ्गाद्वारमुपेयाय मुक्तसर्वानुबन्धनः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaiti jātasunirvedaḥ kṣaṇasaṅgena sādhuṣugaṅgādvāramupeyāya muktasarvānubandhanaḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
In this way, through a moment’s association with the pious (servants of Viṣṇu), he felt a thorough aversion to worldly life (and its pleasures) and freeing himself from all ties (to his mistress, children and property), he proceeded to Gaṅgādvāra.

devanagari: स तस्मिन् देवसदन आसीनो योगमास्थितः । प्रत्याहृतेन्द्रियग्रामो युयोज मन आत्मनि ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: sa tasmin devasadana āsīno yogamāsthitaḥpratyāhṛtendriyagrāmo yuyoja mana ātmani
translation: Settling down in that sacred place (the abode of God), he betook himself to (the path of) Yoga. He withdrew (from worldly pleasures) all his senses and organs, and concentrated his mind on the self.,

devanagari: ततो गुणेभ्य आत्मानं वियुज्यात्मसमाधिना । युयुजे भगवद् धाम्नि ब्रह्मण्यनुभवात्मनि ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: tato guṇebhya ātmānaṃ viyujyātmasamādhināyuyuje bhagavad dhāmni brahmaṇyanubhavātmani
translation: Then, he dissociated his self from (the product of) the guṇas viz. the body, sense-organs etc., and by concentrating his mind, he fixed it on the Supreme Brahman, which is the essential form of the Lord and is the embodiment of consciousness, and bliss.

devanagari: यर्ह्युपारतधीस्तस्मिन् अद्राक्षीत् पुरुषान्पुरः । उपलभ्योपलब्धान् प्राग् ववन्दे शिरसा द्विजः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: yarhyupāratadhīstasmin adrākṣīt puruṣānpuraḥupalabhyopalabdhān prāg vavande śirasā dvijaḥ
translation: When his mind and intellect were thus (thoroughly) Stabilised in the Brahman, the Brāhmaṇa visualized before him those very male figures (messengers of Viṣṇu), and recognizing them as those as having been seen before, he stood up and bowed down his head (to pay respect).

devanagari: हित्वा कलेवरं तीर्थे गङ्गायां दर्शनादनु । सद्यः स्वरूपं जगृहे भगवन् पार्श्ववर्तिनाम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: hitvā kalevaraṃ tīrthe gaṅgāyāṃ darśanādanusadyaḥ svarūpaṃ jagṛhe bhagavan pārśvavartinām
translation: After that vision, he cast off his body at the sacred place (Haridvāra) in the Gaṅgā and immediately assumed the (angelic) form of the attendant of the Lord.

devanagari: साकं विहायसा विप्रो महापुरुषकिङ्करैः । हैमं विमानमारुह्य ययौ यत्र श्रियः पतिः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: sākaṃ vihāyasā vipro mahāpuruṣakiṅkaraiḥhaimaṃ vimānamāruhya yayau yatra śriyaḥ patiḥ
translation: Along with the servants of Lord Viṣṇu, the Brāhmaṇa ascended the heavenly car made of gold and proceeded through heavens to the abode of the Lord of Śrī (i.e. Viṣṇu).

devanagari: एवं स विप्लावितसर्वधर्मा । दास्याः पतिः पतितो गर्ह्यकर्मणा । निपात्यमानो निरये हतव्रतः । सद्यो विमुक्तो भगवन्नाम गृह्णन् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ sa viplāvitasarvadharmādāsyāḥ patiḥ patito garhyakarmaṇānipātyamāno niraye hatavrataḥsadyo vimukto bhagavannāma gṛhṇan
translation: (Ajāmila), the husband of the maid-servant (of a low caste), who had thereby deluged (violated) all religious duties and flouted his vows (of fidelity to his wife etc.) and had (thus) fallen from Brāhmaṇa-hood by his reprehensible acts (like stealing) and was consequently being hurled into the hell (by Yama’s servants), was released (from the noose of Yama) the very moment he uttered the name of the Lord.

devanagari: नातः परं कर्मनिबन्धकृन्तनं । मुमुक्षतां तीर्थपदानुकीर्तनात् । न यत्पुनः कर्मसु सज्जते मनो । रजस्तमोभ्यां कलिलं ततोऽन्यथा ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: nātaḥ paraṃ karmanibandhakṛntanaṃmumukṣatāṃ tīrthapadānukīrtanātna yatpunaḥ karmasu sajjate manorajastamobhyāṃ kalilaṃ tato'nyathā
translation: There is no better efficacious remedy to cut down the roots of sin, for persons wishing Mokṣa (Liberation from Saṃsāra) than the continuous chanting of (the glories and) the name of the Lord of hallowing feet (god Viṣṇu). By having recourse to remembrance (and repetition) of the Lord’s name, the mind is not attached again to acts (karmas); but by any other (expiatory) acts (except the chanting of the Lord’s name) it becomes tainted with rajas and tamas.

devanagari: य एतं परमं गुह्यं इतिहासमघापहम् । श्रृणुयात् श्रद्धया युक्तो यश्च भक्त्यानुकीर्तयेत् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: ya etaṃ paramaṃ guhyaṃ itihāsamaghāpahamśrṛṇuyāt śraddhayā yukto yaśca bhaktyānukīrtayet
translation: He who listens with deep faith and reverence this esoteric legend which is capable of destroying (all) sins and he who devoutly recounts it (to others), never, verily, goes to hell, nor is even eyed by the servants of Yama. Howsoever inauspicious (and sinful) the mortal may have been (in this world) he is greatly honoured in the region of Viṣṇu.

devanagari: न वै स नरकं याति नेक्षितो यमकिङ्करैः । यद्यप्यमङ्गलो मर्त्यो विष्णुलोके महीयते ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: na vai sa narakaṃ yāti nekṣito yamakiṅkaraiḥyadyapyamaṅgalo martyo viṣṇuloke mahīyate
translation: He who listens with deep faith and reverence this esoteric legend which is capable of destroying (all) sins and he who devoutly recounts it (to others), never, verily, goes to hell, nor is even eyed by the servants of Yama. Howsoever inauspicious (and sinful) the mortal may have been (in this world) he is greatly honoured in the region of Viṣṇu.

devanagari: म्रियमाणो हरेर्नाम गृणन् पुत्रोपचारितम् । अजामिलोऽप्यगात् धाम किमुत श्रद्धया गृणन् ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: mriyamāṇo harernāma gṛṇan putropacāritamajāmilo'pyagāt dhāma kimuta śraddhayā gṛṇan
translation:
By (merely) calling out the name of Hari addressed to his son at the time of his own death, (even a sinner like) Ajāmila attained to Lord Viṣṇu’s abode. What should be said of a person who takes his name with faith and devotion. (He would certainly ascend to Viṣṇu’s abode).
[back to top]
